artist.help.disambiguation: Stillwater needs to link this artist to a MusicBrainz entry before it can fetch full metadata. Search for the artist, then click the matching candidate row -- the MusicBrainz ID is saved and future refreshes run without this step.
artist.help.disambiguation.label: Disambiguation
artist.help.images_tab: Each artist has four image slots: Thumb (square portrait), Fanart (wide landscape, multiple allowed), Logo (transparent PNG), and Banner (wide horizontal). Platform terminology differs -- Emby and Jellyfin call Thumb 'Primary' and Fanart 'Backdrop'. Stillwater shows platform labels automatically when your library is linked to a platform profile.
artist.help.images_tab.label: Image slots
artist.help.locked_chip: This artist is locked. Provider refreshes and rule fixers will not make automated changes. Manual edits are still allowed. Click Actions to unlock.
artist.help.locked_chip.label: Artist lock
artist.help.locked_fields_panel: These fields are individually pinned. Even on an unlocked artist, pinned fields are skipped by provider refreshes, fill-empty merges, NFO imports, and snapshot restores. Unlocking the artist does not clear field pins -- they are independent.
artist.help.locked_fields_panel.label: Locked fields
artist.help.refresh_metadata: Pulls current metadata from your configured providers and merges it into this artist record. Locked fields are skipped. Provider priority and merge rules apply -- a refresh does not change which provider supplies which field.
artist.help.refresh_metadata.label: Refresh metadata
artist.help.run_rules: Evaluates every enabled rule against this artist and updates the violations list. Manual-mode rules surface violations for you to act on; auto-mode rules apply fixes immediately.
artist.help.run_rules.label: Run rules
artist.help.severity_column: Severity indicates how strongly the rule flags the issue: info is informational, warning flags issues you should look at, error flags issues that block rule fixers or platform writes. Fix or Dismiss each violation to clear it from the list.
artist.help.severity_column.label: Severity
artist.help.violations_overview: Violations are the output of the rule engine. Each row names the rule that failed, the severity, and the reason. Fixable violations have a Fix button that applies the recommended change; Dismiss closes the violation without changing anything.
artist.help.violations_overview.label: About violations

guide.apikeys_desc: Stillwater provides a REST API at /api/v1/ for programmatic access. API tokens authenticate requests without requiring session cookies.
guide.apikeys_item1: Generate API tokens from Settings > Automation. Each token has a name for identification.
guide.apikeys_item2: Include the token in requests as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.
guide.apikeys_item3: Tokens can be revoked at any time. Revoking a token immediately invalidates all requests using it.
guide.automation_desc: Stillwater can automatically evaluate rules and fix violations on a schedule, reducing manual maintenance.
guide.automation_item1: The rule scheduler runs at a configurable interval (set in Settings > Rules) and evaluates all enabled rules across your library.
guide.automation_item2: Each rule has an automation mode: auto (runs during scheduled evaluation and applies fixes automatically) or manual (requires you to review and fix violations individually).
guide.automation_item3: Auto-fetch can be enabled to automatically retrieve metadata from providers when new artists are discovered during a scan.
guide.automation_item4: Scheduled evaluations and fixes appear in the activity log and can trigger webhook notifications if configured.
guide.conn_emby_item1: Import artist lists from your media server's music libraries
guide.conn_emby_item2: Push metadata updates (biography, genres, sort name) to artist entries
guide.conn_emby_item3: Trigger per-artist or full library refreshes after image and NFO changes
guide.conn_emby_item4: Read MusicBrainz IDs from existing records for accurate provider matching
guide.conn_lidarr_item1: Import monitored artist lists with filesystem paths and MusicBrainz IDs
guide.conn_lidarr_item2: Trigger per-artist metadata refreshes via Lidarr's command queue
guide.conn_lidarr_item3: Detect Lidarr NFO writer configuration to warn about write conflicts
guide.conn_lidarr_item4: Read metadata profiles for configuration awareness
guide.connections_desc: Connections let Stillwater communicate with your media server or music manager. They are optional and can be added at any time from Settings.
guide.connections_note: Each connection requires a server URL and API key. Use the "Test" button after saving to verify connectivity.
guide.image_banner_desc: Horizontal banner image. Used in some media server skins for top-of-page display. Typically JPG, but PNG is preserved when the source is PNG.
guide.image_fanart_desc: Wide background image. Used as the backdrop on artist detail pages. Typically JPG, but PNG is preserved when the source is PNG.
guide.image_logo_desc: Transparent artist logo/wordmark. Always saved as PNG to preserve the alpha channel.
guide.image_thumb_desc: Primary artist portrait. Used in list views, grids, and search results. Typically JPG, but PNG is preserved when the source is PNG.
guide.images_comparison_desc: Compare your local images against results from metadata providers. The side-by-side view shows differences between what you have and what is available, letting you choose which version to keep.
guide.images_cropping_desc: Crop any image before saving it to your library. The built-in cropping tool lets you adjust the visible area and aspect ratio to match your platform's requirements.
guide.images_desc: Stillwater manages four image types per artist. Each type serves a different purpose in your media server's UI.
guide.images_item1: Supported input formats: JPEG, PNG, and WebP. WebP images are converted to PNG on save.
guide.images_item2: Logos are always saved as PNG (to preserve transparency). Other types retain their decoded format (JPG or PNG).
guide.images_item3: When saving a new image, any existing file of the same type in a different format is automatically deleted to prevent duplicates.
guide.images_item4: Images can be fetched from providers, uploaded manually, or found via web image search.
guide.images_websearch_desc: Search the web for artist images using DuckDuckGo when provider results are insufficient. Web search supports thumb, fanart, logo, and banner image types.
guide.library_structure_desc: Stillwater expects each top-level directory in your music library to represent a single album artist. The scanner reads these directory names to build the artist list.
guide.library_structure_item1: One album artist per top-level directory. Nested album folders inside each artist directory are fine.
guide.library_structure_item2: Classical libraries can use a separate library with the "Classical" type, which adjusts scanner behavior for composer-oriented structures.
guide.library_structure_item3: Compilation directories ("Various Artists", "Soundtrack", "OST") lack meaningful artist metadata and are excluded by default. See {link} below.
guide.nfo_desc: NFO files are XML-formatted metadata files placed in each artist's directory. Media servers read these files to display artist information.
guide.nfo_item1: Each NFO file contains biography, sort name, MusicBrainz ID, genres, styles, moods, formation/disbandment years, and band members.
guide.nfo_item2: NFO files are written to the artist's directory using the atomic write pattern: write to a temporary file, rename the original to a backup, then rename the temporary file into place.
guide.nfo_item3: Before overwriting, Stillwater checks the file's last-modified timestamp. If it was changed externally (e.g., by Kodi or another tool), a warning is shown instead of silently overwriting.
guide.oobe_auth_desc: Choose how users sign in. You can create a local account with a username and password, or authenticate through an existing Emby or Jellyfin server.
guide.oobe_complete: After completing setup, you arrive at the Artists page where you can browse, search, and manage your artist metadata.
guide.oobe_desc: Follow these steps to set up Stillwater for the first time.
guide.oobe_step1: Add your music library paths. Each library is a root folder containing artist directories.
guide.oobe_step2: Select your platform profile (Kodi, Emby, or Jellyfin). This determines image file naming conventions.
guide.oobe_step3: Configure API keys for metadata providers. Free providers work immediately; others require keys from their respective sites.
guide.oobe_step4: Set up server connections to sync artist data with your media platform.
guide.oobe_step5: Run the initial scan to discover artists from your library directories.
guide.platform_profiles_desc: Platform profiles control how Stillwater writes NFO files and names image files. Each media server expects a slightly different format.
guide.platform_profiles_note: Only one profile is active at a time. Switching profiles changes how new images and NFO files are written. You can change the active profile from the Settings page or during onboarding.
guide.provider_audiodb_desc: Biographies, genres, styles, moods, formation year, artist images. Premium keys unlock higher rate limits.
guide.provider_deezer_desc: Artist images, basic metadata. Useful as a fallback image source when Fanart.tv has no results.
guide.provider_discogs_desc: Detailed artist profiles, member lists, name variations. Useful for electronic and niche genres.
guide.provider_duckduckgo_desc: Web image search for finding artist photos and backgrounds when provider results are insufficient. Supports thumb, fanart, logo, and banner image types.
guide.provider_fanarttv_desc: High-quality artist images: thumbs, fanart, logos, banners. The primary image source for most users.
guide.provider_genius_desc: Artist biographies and alternate names. Requires an API key from the Genius developer portal.
guide.provider_lastfm_desc: Biographies, tags (used as genres/styles), similar artists. Good fallback for biography text.
guide.provider_musicbrainz_desc: Artist identifiers (MBIDs), basic metadata, disambiguation, relationships. The foundation for cross-referencing other providers.
guide.provider_spotify_desc: Artist images. Uses OAuth client credentials for access.
guide.provider_wikidata_desc: Structured data linked from MusicBrainz: formation date, country, genres, official website URLs.
guide.provider_wikipedia_desc: Biographies, band members, genres, years active, and country of origin parsed from Wikipedia articles linked via Wikidata.
guide.providers_desc: Stillwater pulls artist metadata from multiple sources. Each provider offers different data, and you can configure which provider wins for each field via the priority system in Settings.
guide.rules_desc: Rules define quality checks that Stillwater runs against your artist library. When an artist violates a rule, a notification is created so you can review and fix the issue.
guide.rules_fix_image: Image fixer fetches missing images from configured metadata providers.
guide.rules_fix_metadata: Metadata fixer retrieves missing fields (biography, genres, dates) from providers.
guide.rules_fix_nfo: NFO fixer creates missing artist.nfo files from existing metadata.
guide.rules_fixes_desc: When a rule violation is found, Stillwater can attempt an automatic fix.
guide.rules_item1: Rules check for missing metadata fields (biography, genres), missing images, and data quality issues.
guide.rules_item2: Each rule can be individually enabled or disabled from the Settings page.
guide.rules_item3: Violations can be dismissed (ignored), resolved (fixed), or auto-fixed when a candidate value is available.
guide.rules_item4: Rules can be run manually on a single artist or in bulk across the entire library.
guide.rules_item5: Webhook notifications can be configured to fire when new violations are detected.
guide.rules_notifications_desc: Configure webhook notifications to receive alerts when rule violations are found or resolved. Webhooks can be set up in Settings to post to services like Discord, Slack, or any URL that accepts JSON payloads.
guide.rules_type_image: Image rules verify that required images exist, meet minimum resolution, have correct aspect ratios, and detect duplicates or extraneous files.
guide.rules_type_metadata: Metadata rules check for biography presence, artist ID consistency, directory name matching, and overall metadata quality.
guide.rules_type_nfo: NFO rules check for artist.nfo file presence and required fields like MusicBrainz ID.
guide.rules_types_desc: Rules are organized into three categories based on what they check.
guide.scanner_exclusions_desc: The scanner skips directories that match known compilation or soundtrack folder names. These directories contain albums from multiple artists and lack the single-artist metadata that Stillwater manages.
guide.scanner_exclusions_item1: Excluded directories appear greyed out in the artist list and cannot be fetched.
guide.scanner_exclusions_item3: Exclusions can be customized using the SW_SCANNER_EXCLUSIONS environment variable. There is no Settings UI for exclusions.
guide.scanner_item_conn_import: Artists can also be imported from a connected Emby, Jellyfin, or Lidarr server via the Connections page.
guide.scanner_item_fs_scan: Filesystem scan reads artist directory names from your configured music library paths.
guide.settings_auth_desc: Configure external authentication via OIDC, Emby, or Jellyfin SSO.
guide.settings_automation_desc: Manage API tokens, webhooks, and notification badge behavior.
guide.settings_connections_desc: Manage server connections to Emby, Jellyfin, and Lidarr.
guide.settings_desc: The Settings page organizes configuration into tabs. Access it from the sidebar navigation.
guide.settings_general_desc: Application name, base URL, language, and display preferences.
guide.settings_libraries_desc: Add and manage music library paths that Stillwater scans for artists.
guide.settings_logs_desc: View application logs, change log level, and configure log file rotation.
guide.settings_maintenance_desc: Database backups, cache management, and data cleanup tools.
guide.settings_providers_desc: Configure API keys for metadata providers and set provider priority order.
guide.settings_rules_desc: Enable or disable individual rules and set each rule's automation mode.
guide.settings_users_desc: Manage user accounts, change passwords, and assign roles.
guide.subtitle: Reference documentation for configuring and using Stillwater.
guide.title: User Guide
guide.tour_step_artist_list_desc: Your artists appear here after scanning. Click any artist to view and edit their metadata, images, and platform connections.
guide.tour_step_filter_desc: Narrow results by metadata status, image presence, artist type, or library.
guide.tour_step_nav_desc: Use the sidebar to switch between Dashboard, Artists, Reports, and Settings. Collapse it with the arrow at the top for more space.
guide.tour_step_scan_desc: Click here to scan your music folders. Stillwater will discover artists and fetch their metadata automatically.
guide.tour_step_search_desc: Type a name to instantly filter your artist list.
guide.tour_step_sort_desc: Reorder by name, health score, date added, or last updated.
guide.tour_step_view_desc: Toggle between a detailed table view and a visual grid view.
guide.tour_step_dash_search_desc: Filter the action queue by artist name to quickly find items that need attention.
guide.tour_step_dash_run_rules_desc: Evaluate every rule against every artist in one pass. Issues that can be auto-fixed are resolved immediately; others are queued below for review.
guide.tour_step_dash_queue_desc: Artists with rule violations or metadata gaps appear here. Click an item to jump to that artist and fix the issue.
guide.tour_step_dash_activity_desc: Recent Stillwater activity -- scans, metadata fetches, and publishes -- streams here in real time.
guide.tour_step_detail_hero_desc: The artist name, type, and primary artwork are shown here. Click the artwork to open the image manager.
guide.tour_step_detail_refresh_desc: Pull fresh metadata from your configured providers. You can choose which fields to update before confirming.
guide.tour_step_detail_findings_desc: Rule violations specific to this artist appear here. Fix or dismiss each finding individually, or run all rules at once.

onboarding.compilation_exclusions: Compilation folders like "Various Artists", "Various", "VA", "Soundtrack", and "OST" are excluded by default since they lack meaningful artist metadata.

onboarding.conflict_check_failed: Could not reach the server to check for write-back conflicts.
onboarding.conflict_check_intro: Stillwater can check whether your media servers will fight Stillwater for control of artwork and NFO files in your music library. This takes a few seconds.
onboarding.conflict_checking: Checking your servers for write-back conflicts...
onboarding.conflict_clean: No conflicts detected. Stillwater and your media servers will not duplicate files.
onboarding.conflict_image_saver_on: is configured to save image files into your library directory.
onboarding.conflict_nfo_writer_on: is configured to write artist.nfo into your library directory.
onboarding.conflict_round_trip: Two of your connections share library paths. Resolve before continuing.
onboarding.conflict_round_trip_blocks_co: Continue is disabled until the round-trip overlap is resolved.
onboarding.connections_description: Optionally connect to your media server or music manager. These are not required for core functionality and can be added later from Settings.
onboarding.discovery_cancel_hint: Already-linked artists are saved immediately. You can cancel at any time without losing progress.
onboarding.discovery_check_failed: Could not check artist identification status
onboarding.discovery_check_failed_hint: You can still start discovery -- it will determine the count automatically.
onboarding.discovery_compare_albums: Compares local album folders against MusicBrainz discographies
onboarding.discovery_description: Stillwater can search your configured providers to identify artists in your library and fetch metadata automatically.
onboarding.discovery_match_libraries: Matches artists against your Emby/Jellyfin/Lidarr libraries first
onboarding.discovery_queue_review: Queues uncertain matches for your review
onboarding.discovery_skip_hint: You can skip this and identify artists individually later from the Artists page.
onboarding.emby_import_artists: Import artist lists from your Emby music libraries
onboarding.emby_push_metadata: Push metadata updates (biography, genres, sort name) to Emby artist entries
onboarding.emby_read_mbids: Read MusicBrainz IDs from existing Emby records for accurate provider matching
onboarding.emby_trigger_refresh: Trigger per-artist or full library refreshes after image and NFO changes
onboarding.feature_images_desc: Download and manage artist thumbnails, fanart, logos, and banners. Compare options side-by-side, crop to fit, and save in the correct format for your platform.
onboarding.feature_metadata_desc: Fetch biographies, genres, styles, moods, and formation dates from multiple sources and write them as standard NFO files your media server can read.
onboarding.feature_platforms_desc: Choose your media server (Emby, Jellyfin, or Kodi) and Stillwater adapts NFO format and image naming conventions to match what it expects.
onboarding.feature_providers_desc: Pull data from MusicBrainz, Fanart.tv, TheAudioDB, Last.fm, Discogs, Wikidata, and Deezer. Configure priority order per field so the best source wins.
onboarding.feature_rules_desc: Define rules that automatically apply metadata decisions across your library. Set genre mappings, image preferences, and field overrides that run on every fetch.
onboarding.feature_scanner_desc: Directories named "Various Artists", "VA", "Soundtrack", or "OST" are excluded from scanning by default. These compilation folders lack meaningful artist metadata.
onboarding.help.conflict_preflight: Stillwater probes your connected media servers to detect write-back conflicts -- settings that would cause the server to overwrite Stillwater's NFO or image files. A red result blocks the Continue button until you resolve the overlap, typically by disabling the server's local saver or using the "Let Stillwater manage" toggle.
onboarding.help.conflict_preflight.label: Conflict pre-flight
onboarding.help.connections: Connecting Emby or Jellyfin lets Stillwater import the server's artist library, push metadata edits, and trigger post-change refreshes. Lidarr connections are read-only (no library import). Each connection needs a server URL and an API key from the server's admin UI. Connections are optional -- NFO writeback works without them.
onboarding.help.connections.label: Connections
onboarding.help.discovery: Discovery walks the artists in your library and asks providers to identify each one. High-confidence matches are linked automatically. Ambiguous matches go to a review queue you can work through after the wizard; the rest stay unidentified for later manual linking.
onboarding.help.discovery.label: Artist discovery
onboarding.help.platform: The platform profile controls the NFO field set and image-naming conventions Stillwater uses when writing to disk. Pick the media server you primarily target -- Emby, Jellyfin, Kodi, or Plex. You can change this later under Settings.
onboarding.help.platform.label: Platform profile
onboarding.help.providers: Providers are the metadata and image sources Stillwater queries: MusicBrainz, Fanart.tv, Last.fm, Discogs, Genius, Spotify, Wikipedia, Wikidata, AudioDB, and others. Most that require an API key are free; Spotify needs a paid Spotify Premium account. Keys can be added later under Settings -- Stillwater runs on keyless providers in the meantime.
onboarding.help.providers.label: Providers
onboarding.intro_description: Stillwater manages artist metadata and images for your self-hosted music library. It writes NFO files and organizes artwork so your media server displays rich artist information.
onboarding.jellyfin_import_artists: Import artist lists from your Jellyfin music libraries
onboarding.jellyfin_push_metadata: Push metadata updates (biography, genres, sort name) to Jellyfin artist entries
onboarding.jellyfin_read_mbids: Read MusicBrainz IDs from existing Jellyfin records for accurate provider matching
onboarding.jellyfin_trigger_refresh: Trigger per-artist or full library refreshes after image and NFO changes
onboarding.libraries_description: Add your music library paths. Each library maps to a directory containing artist folders.
onboarding.lidarr_detect_nfo: Detect Lidarr NFO writer configuration to warn about write conflicts
onboarding.lidarr_no_library: No library is created for Lidarr connections
onboarding.lidarr_read_profiles: Read metadata profiles for configuration awareness
onboarding.lidarr_readonly: Read-only metadata source for MusicBrainz ID seeding
onboarding.no_mbid_linked: These artists have no MusicBrainz ID linked. Discovery will search providers and auto-link high-confidence matches.
onboarding.nothing_to_discover: Nothing to discover. You're all set!
onboarding.platform_description: Select the media server you use. This controls NFO format and image naming conventions.
onboarding.providers_description: Configure API keys for metadata and image providers. You can skip this and add keys later from Settings.
onboarding.review_instructions: Click an artist to see candidates. Pick the correct match or skip. Unreviewed artists can be identified later from the Artists page.
onboarding.language_body: We use these languages, in order, when fetching artist biographies and descriptions from metadata providers. Search and pick the languages you want; drag pills to reorder priority.
onboarding.language_skip_consequence: We'll use English for all metadata until you change this in Settings.
onboarding.baseline_body: We found %d existing artwork files in your music library. Treat them as a baseline so future changes are flagged as drift?
onboarding.baseline_yes: Yes -- baseline these files (recommended). Future changes from this point will be flagged.
onboarding.baseline_no: No -- treat them as already-flagged drift.
onboarding.web_search_description: Enable web search providers to find additional artist images via search engines. No API key required.

report.confirm_run_all_body:
This forces a full re-evaluation of every eligible artist, ignoring the incremental dirty tracker.

Use this only when you need a clean baseline -- the normal Run Rules button is much faster because it skips artists that have not changed.
report.confirm_run_all_body_estimate:
This forces a full re-evaluation of {artists} artists and may take approximately {duration}.

Use this only when you need a clean baseline -- the normal Run Rules button is much faster because it skips artists that have not changed.
report.confirm_run_body:
This will re-evaluate rules for every artist that has changed since the last run. Unchanged artists are skipped.

The evaluation runs in the background -- you can continue using Stillwater while it runs.
report.confirm_run_body_estimate:
This will re-evaluate rules for up to {artists} artists and may take approximately {duration}. Unchanged artists are skipped, so the actual run is often much faster.

The evaluation runs in the background -- you can continue using Stillwater while it runs.

rules.discography.coverage_threshold.hel: An artist.nfo is flagged when its album entries cover fewer than this percentage of the MusicBrainz release groups for the selected release types. Lower the value to flag only severely incomplete discographies; raise it to flag any artist whose NFO trails MusicBrainz.
rules.discography.release_types.help: Comma-separated MusicBrainz primary types to fetch and count (for example: Album,EP). Coverage detection and the fix both honor this list, so types you exclude here are neither flagged nor merged.
settings.active_profile.description: A platform profile bundles the NFO format and image filename conventions for one media platform (Emby, Jellyfin, or Kodi). This card shows what the active profile will write and lets you override the filename used for each image type. Edits are saved back to the profile.
settings.active_profile.help: Override the filenames used for poster, banner, logo, and other image types in the active platform profile. The profile's defaults are pre-filled; clearing a field restores the default.
settings.active_profile.nfo_output.descr: NFO files are XML metadata sidecars that Emby, Jellyfin, and Kodi read alongside artist folders to display biography, MusicBrainz ID, and other curated values. This row reflects the active platform profile's NFO output choice: whether Stillwater writes them at all, and in which format. Edit the profile to change.
settings.active_profile.save_filenames.d: Image filenames are how each platform discovers cover art on disk (folder.jpg, fanart.jpg, banner.jpg, and so on). Saves the per-image-type filenames you edited above into the active profile so future image writes land at those paths.
settings.active_profile.title: Active Profile Details
settings.advanced.description: Provider matching is how Stillwater decides whether a search result returned by MusicBrainz, Discogs, or another source actually refers to the artist you asked about. These controls adjust the thresholds and tie-breakers used during that match.
settings.advanced.help: Tuning knobs for how Stillwater decides whether a provider response matches the local artist. Defaults are sensible for most libraries; raise these only if you see false matches, lower them only if real matches are getting rejected.
settings.advanced.title: Advanced
settings.api_tokens.confirm_delete: Permanently delete this token? This cannot be undone. Audit log entries will be anonymized.
settings.api_tokens.confirm_revoke: Revoke this API token? Applications using it will lose access.
settings.api_tokens.created_message: Token created. Copy it now -- it will not be shown again.
settings.api_tokens.description: API tokens are long-lived credentials that let scripts and external tools call the Stillwater REST API without a browser session. Each token is scoped (read, write, webhook, or admin) so you can grant exactly the access an integration needs and revoke it independently.
settings.api_tokens.help: Bearer tokens that let scripts and external integrations call the Stillwater API. Each token has a scope that controls what it can do (read-only, write, webhook ingress, or admin). Tokens are shown once at creation; if you lose one, revoke it and create a new one.
settings.api_tokens.scope_admin.descript: Grants every API scope (read, write, and webhook) on top of the routes the owning user's role allows. Routes that are gated by the administrator role still require the owning user to be an administrator; revocation is always limited to the owning user's own tokens.
settings.api_tokens.scope_admin.help: Combines read, write, and webhook scopes. Admin-gated routes still require the owning user to hold the administrator role.
settings.api_tokens.scope_read.descripti: Read-only access to artists, libraries, rules, and settings. Safe for dashboards and one-way integrations that only fetch data.
settings.api_tokens.scope_read.help: Read-only access to artists, libraries, rules, and settings. Safe for dashboards and integrations that only consume data.
settings.api_tokens.scope_webhook.descri: Lets the token receive inbound webhook deliveries from external systems. Pair with a single integration so an exposed token does not also grant read or write access.
settings.api_tokens.scope_webhook.help: Allows the token to receive inbound webhook deliveries. Use a dedicated token with only this scope to limit blast radius if the token is ever exposed.
settings.api_tokens.scope_write.descript: Create and modify artists, run rules, and queue background work. Use this for automation scripts that need to make changes but should not touch user accounts.
settings.api_tokens.scope_write.help: Permits creating and modifying artists, triggering rule fixes, and queuing background work. Does not grant access to user management or admin-only settings.
settings.api_tokens.title: API Tokens
settings.appearance.accessibility_and_pe: The default UI uses animations, frosted-glass blur, and BlurHash placeholders that are visually rich but expensive to render. Settings here let you trade those effects away for steadier performance on low-power devices or to honor accessibility needs like reduced motion.
settings.appearance.behavior.description: Settings here decide what Stillwater does on its own when you open an artist screen, versus what it waits for you to ask for. The current behavior knob is whether image search runs automatically when you visit an artist's image page.
settings.appearance.behavior.label: Behavior
settings.appearance.bg_opacity.descripti: Stillwater renders cards and modals as frosted-glass panels: a translucent tint over a blurred copy of whatever sits behind them. This slider sets that tint's opacity. Lower values let more of the page background bleed through; higher values approach a solid panel.
settings.appearance.bg_opacity.help: Sets the opacity of the colored background layer behind frosted glass panels. Lower values make panels more transparent, higher values make them more opaque.
settings.appearance.bg_opacity.label: Background Opacity
settings.appearance.content_width.descri: Pages render inside a content column whose width is configurable. Narrow caps the column at a comfortable reading measure regardless of monitor size; Full lets the column expand all the way to the viewport edges so wide tables and image grids use every available pixel.
settings.appearance.content_width.label: Content Width
settings.appearance.font_family.descript: The font family is the typeface Stillwater uses for body copy, table rows, and UI labels. Pick the one that reads most comfortably for you; the choice applies across every page.
settings.appearance.font_family.label: Font Family
settings.appearance.font_license: Inter and Atkinson Hyperlegible are bundled under the SIL Open Font License 1.1.
settings.appearance.font_size.descriptio: Every text size in the app scales off a single base size. Increasing it makes paragraphs, table cells, and labels uniformly larger; decreasing it fits more content on screen at the cost of legibility.
settings.appearance.font_size.label: Font Size
settings.appearance.language.description: Stillwater is internationalized through translation files keyed per locale, so labels, errors, and help text can render in any language a translation exists for. English is the only locale shipped today; additional languages will appear here as they land.
settings.appearance.language.label: Language
settings.appearance.language_section.des: The language section is where the locale that drives every UI string lives. Today only English ships, but the section is split out so additional translations can slot in without restructuring the page when they arrive.
settings.appearance.layout.description: Layout settings decide the geometry of the page: how wide the content column gets, how the sidebar opens, and how big the artist image cards are. Adjust them to match your screen size and how dense you want each page to feel.
settings.appearance.layout.label: Layout
settings.appearance.letter_spacing.descr: Letter spacing (also called tracking) is the gap between adjacent characters within words. Adding spacing can make dense paragraphs and long artist names easier to scan; tightening it packs more text into the same width.
settings.appearance.letter_spacing.help: Increases the space between characters. Wide and Extra Wide are useful for users with dyslexia or low vision. Each option is rendered in its own spacing so you can preview the difference.
settings.appearance.letter_spacing.label: Letter Spacing
settings.appearance.lite_mode.descriptio: Lite mode is a single switch that turns off every visually expensive feature at once: frosted-glass blur, transitions, BlurHash placeholders that render before images load, and full-resolution image fetching. Use it if pages feel sluggish or if you would rather see thumbnails immediately than wait for full-size art.
settings.appearance.lite_mode.help: Auto detects low-end devices (fewer than 4 CPU cores or less than 4 GB RAM) and enables Lite Mode automatically. On forces Lite Mode regardless of device, improving performance on constrained hardware or slow connections.
settings.appearance.lite_mode.label: Lite Mode
settings.appearance.metadata_lang_hint: To set preferred languages for artist metadata, visit {link}.
settings.appearance.page.description: Appearance preferences are stored per user account, so each person who logs into this Stillwater can have their own theme, typography, and layout. The settings on this page only affect what you see and do not change anything for other users.
settings.appearance.page_size.descriptio: Long lists in Stillwater (the artists grid, activity feed, action queue) are paginated rather than infinite-scrolled. This setting picks how many rows or cards each page holds before you have to advance to the next.
settings.appearance.page_size.label: Page Size
settings.appearance.reduced_motion.descr: Some users find page transitions and animated state changes distracting or motion-sensitivity inducing. Turning this on suppresses or removes those animations across the app, so panels swap and toasts appear instantly instead of fading.
settings.appearance.reduced_motion.help: System follows your OS-level accessibility setting. On forces reduced motion regardless of OS preference, which is useful if your browser does not expose the system setting.
settings.appearance.reduced_motion.label: Reduced Motion
settings.appearance.sidebar_default_stat: The sidebar holds the primary navigation and can be collapsed to icons-only or expanded to show labels. This setting picks which state you land on every time you load Stillwater; you can still toggle it during a session.
settings.appearance.theme.description: The theme is the overall color scheme: background, panel, and accent colors all derive from it. Light, Dark, and System (follow the operating system) are the available choices.
settings.appearance.theme.help: Light uses a bright background. Dark uses a dark background. System follows your device's color scheme preference and changes automatically.
settings.appearance.theme.label: Theme
settings.appearance.theme_and_color.desc: These settings own the visual identity of the interface: which color scheme is in effect (light, dark, or follow-system) and how heavily the frosted-glass tint sits on top of panels. Adjust them together to balance contrast and depth.
settings.appearance.thumbnail_size.descr: The artists list and image grids render each entry as a card with cover art on top. Thumbnail size sets how big those cards are: smaller fits more artists per row, larger makes the artwork more prominent at the cost of scrolling.
settings.appearance.thumbnail_size.label: Thumbnail Size
settings.appearance.typography.descripti: Typography controls how text is rendered everywhere in the app: which font is used, how big the body size is, and how tightly characters sit next to each other. Adjust these together to dial in legibility for your screen.
settings.appearance.typography.label: Typography
settings.auth.admin_groups.description: Comma-separated list of OIDC groups whose members are granted the Administrator role. Stillwater reads the values from the groups claim on the ID token.
settings.auth.admin_groups.help: Comma-separated list of OIDC group claim values. Users in any of these groups are granted the Administrator role at sign-in. The claim is read from the 'groups' field in the ID token.
settings.auth.admin_groups.label: OIDC Admin Groups
settings.auth.admin_groups_aria: OIDC group claims that grant Administrator role (comma-separated)
settings.auth.admin_groups_hint: OIDC group claims that grant Administrator role (comma-separated)
settings.auth.admins_only.description: Only users with an existing admin account in the upstream provider are allowed to auto-provision. Other users can still sign in but will not have a Stillwater account created for them.
settings.auth.allowed_groups.description: Comma-separated list of OIDC groups allowed to log in. Leave empty to allow every authenticated user from this provider.
settings.auth.allowed_groups.help: Comma-separated list of groups allowed to log in. Leave empty to allow all authenticated users from this provider. Users not in any listed group will be denied access.
settings.auth.allowed_groups.label: OIDC Allowed Groups
settings.auth.allowed_groups_aria: Restrict OIDC login to these groups (comma-separated, empty allows all)
settings.auth.allowed_groups_hint: Restrict login to these groups. Leave empty to allow all authenticated users.
settings.auth.any_user.description: Every user the upstream provider authenticates is auto-provisioned a Stillwater account at the configured default role.
settings.auth.auto_provision_emby.descri: Auto-provisioning means Stillwater creates a local account on the fly the first time someone signs in through the upstream provider. With this on, anyone who can authenticate against the linked Emby server gets a Stillwater account without an admin issuing an invite first; the guard rail below decides who actually qualifies.
settings.auth.auto_provision_emby.label: Auto-Provision
settings.auth.auto_provision_jellyfin.de: Auto-provisioning means Stillwater creates a local account on the fly the first time someone signs in through the upstream provider. With this on, anyone who can authenticate against the linked Jellyfin server gets a Stillwater account without an admin issuing an invite first; the guard rail below decides who actually qualifies.
settings.auth.auto_provision_oidc.descri: Auto-provisioning means Stillwater creates a local account on the fly the first time someone signs in. With this on, every user the OIDC provider authenticates gets a Stillwater account without an admin issuing an invite first; the allowed groups list below restricts who qualifies.
settings.auth.auto_provision_oidc.label: Auto-Provision
settings.auth.client_id.description: Public identifier registered for Stillwater in your OIDC provider.
settings.auth.client_id.help: The public identifier for Stillwater registered in your identity provider. Used with the issuer URL to start the authorization flow.
settings.auth.client_id.label: OIDC Client ID
settings.auth.client_secret.description: Confidential credential issued by your OIDC provider. Leave blank when editing other fields to keep the existing secret.
settings.auth.client_secret.help: Confidential credential from your identity provider. Leave blank when editing to keep the currently stored secret unchanged.
settings.auth.client_secret.label: OIDC Client Secret
settings.auth.default_role.description: Stillwater accounts have a role (Administrator or User) that decides what they can change. This setting picks the role assigned to brand-new accounts created by auto-provisioning; an admin can promote or demote them later.
settings.auth.default_role.label: Default Role
settings.auth.default_role_oidc: Default role for OIDC users not in an admin group
settings.auth.description: An authentication provider is the system Stillwater asks to verify a user's password before letting them in: a local username/password, an Emby or Jellyfin server, or an OIDC identity provider. Enable as many as you want; users see a sign-in button per enabled provider.
settings.auth.emby.description: When this provider is on, the sign-in form asks for the username and password of an account on the linked Emby server and verifies them against Emby's API rather than against Stillwater's own user table. Reuses whichever Emby connection you have already configured.
settings.auth.emby.label: Emby
settings.auth.enable_auto_provision_emby: When enabled, Stillwater creates a local account the first time someone signs in through Emby. The guard rail below controls whether only Emby admins or all Emby users qualify.
settings.auth.enable_auto_provision_jell: When enabled, Stillwater creates a local account the first time someone signs in through Jellyfin. The guard rail below controls whether only Jellyfin admins or all Jellyfin users qualify.
settings.auth.enable_emby.help: When enabled, a sign-in button for Emby appears on the login page. Users enter their Emby credentials and Stillwater verifies them against the Emby server URL configured below (typically set automatically when you add an Emby connection on the Connections tab).
settings.auth.enable_emby.label: Enable Emby Auth
settings.auth.enable_jellyfin.help: When enabled, a sign-in button for Jellyfin appears on the login page. Users enter their Jellyfin credentials and Stillwater verifies them against the Jellyfin server URL configured below (typically set automatically when you add a Jellyfin connection on the Connections tab).
settings.auth.enable_jellyfin.label: Enable Jellyfin Auth
settings.auth.enable_oidc.help: When enabled, a sign-in button for your OIDC provider appears on the login page. Users are redirected to the provider to authenticate and returned to Stillwater on success. Requires the Issuer URL and Client ID fields below.
settings.auth.enable_oidc.label: Enable OIDC Auth
settings.auth.groups_placeholder: Leave empty to allow all users
settings.auth.guard_rail.description: When auto-provisioning is on, the guard rail narrows who actually gets an account created. Pick admins-only to limit it to users with admin rights on the upstream Emby or Jellyfin server, or any user to provision everyone the provider authenticates.
settings.auth.guard_rail.label: Guard Rail
settings.auth.help: Auth providers control who can sign in to Stillwater. Local accounts are always on as a break-glass fallback; Emby, Jellyfin, and OIDC can each be enabled separately so users from those systems can log in with their existing credentials.
settings.auth.issuer_url.description: Base URL of your OIDC provider. Stillwater discovers the rest of the endpoints automatically through the provider's well-known configuration document.
settings.auth.issuer_url.help: The base URL of your OpenID Connect provider (e.g. https://accounts.google.com). Stillwater appends the well-known configuration path to discover authorization and token endpoints automatically.
settings.auth.jellyfin.description: When this provider is on, the sign-in form asks for the username and password of an account on the linked Jellyfin server and verifies them against Jellyfin's API rather than against Stillwater's own user table. Requires an active Jellyfin connection.
settings.auth.jellyfin.label: Jellyfin
settings.auth.local.description: Local accounts live in Stillwater's own user table: username, role, and a password hash that Stillwater verifies itself with no external service involved. This is the simplest provider to enable and is on by default for the first admin account.
settings.auth.local.help: Local authentication cannot be disabled. It provides break-glass access if all other providers are misconfigured.
settings.auth.local.label: Local
settings.auth.oidc.description: OpenID Connect (OIDC) is a standard protocol that lets Stillwater redirect sign-in to an existing identity provider so users authenticate there once and reach every connected app without re-entering credentials. Works with Authentik, Keycloak, Authelia, Auth0, or any OIDC-compliant provider.
settings.auth.oidc.label: OpenID Connect (OIDC)
settings.auth.oidc_auto_provision.help: When enabled, a Stillwater account is automatically created the first time an OIDC user logs in. When disabled, an administrator must create the account manually before the user can log in.
settings.auth.oidc_auto_provision.label: OIDC Auto-Provision
settings.auth.oidc_display_name.descript: Provider name shown on the sign-in button. Falls back to a generic OIDC label when blank.
settings.auth.oidc_display_name_hint: Provider name shown on the sign-in button, e.g. "Authentik". Defaults to "Sign in with OIDC" when empty.
settings.auth.oidc_issuer.help: The base URL of your OIDC provider (Authentik, Keycloak, Authelia, etc.). Stillwater uses this to discover endpoints via the .well-known/openid-configuration document.
settings.auth.oidc_issuer.label: OIDC Issuer URL
settings.auth.oidc_logo_url.description: Optional image shown next to the OIDC sign-in button. A key icon is used when blank.
settings.auth.oidc_logo_url_hint: Optional image URL shown next to the sign-in button. Leave empty to use the default key icon.
settings.auth.title: Authentication Providers
settings.auto_fetch.description: When opening an artist's image management page, automatically search providers for available images. This applies to manual browsing only and does not affect rule-based image fetching.
settings.auto_fetch.title: Auto-fetch images
settings.backup.description: A backup is a snapshot of Stillwater's SQLite database, which holds every artist record, library configuration, rule, and setting on this instance. Use this section to take an on-demand backup, download an existing one for off-instance storage, or restore a snapshot if something goes wrong.
settings.backup.help: Database backups are timestamped copies of Stillwater's SQLite database written to disk. They run on a schedule and let you roll back if a migration, rule fix, or accidental import breaks something; older backups are pruned automatically based on the retention rules below.
settings.backup.keep.description: Maximum number of backups to retain. Older backups are pruned after each automatic backup once this count is exceeded.
settings.backup.keep.help: Maximum number of backup files to retain on disk. Once this count is exceeded, the oldest backups are removed automatically after each new backup.
settings.backup.max_age.description: Discard automatic backups older than the selected age. Set to Never to keep backups indefinitely; the count limit still applies.
settings.backup.max_age.help: Backup files older than this age are removed automatically. Set to Never to rely only on the count limit. Both limits apply; whichever is reached first triggers pruning.
settings.backup.retention.description: Controls how long Stillwater keeps automatic backups before pruning them.
settings.backup.retention_note: Oldest backups are pruned after each automatic backup when they exceed the configured retention count or maximum age.
settings.backup.title: Database Backup
settings.backup_schedule.description: How often Stillwater takes an automatic database backup.
settings.backup_schedule.help: Sets the interval between automatic database backups (the SW_BACKUP_INTERVAL setting). The backup scheduler starts when Stillwater launches, so a new interval takes effect after the next restart.
settings.backup_schedule.interval.descri: Number of hours between automatic backups. Must be a positive whole number.
settings.backup_schedule.interval.label: Backup Interval
settings.backup_schedule.restart_require: Restart Stillwater for the new backup interval to take effect. The backup scheduler is started once at launch and does not pick up the change while running.
Restart required to apply the new backup interval
settings.backup_schedule.title: Backup Schedule
settings.base_path.config_hint: Edit and save to override the YAML config value. Stillwater must be restarted for the change to take effect.
settings.base_path.description: URL path prefix for running Stillwater behind a reverse proxy at a sub-path.
settings.base_path.env_override: Currently set by the SW_BASE_PATH environment variable. The environment variable takes precedence over any value saved here.
settings.base_path.error_charset: Base path may only contain letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores, and slashes.
settings.base_path.error_must_not_end_sl: Base path must not end with "/".
settings.base_path.error_protocol_relati: Base path must not start with "//" or "/\\".
settings.base_path.help: Set this when Stillwater lives at a sub-path (for example /stillwater) behind a reverse proxy rather than at the domain root. Must start with a slash and not end with one. Stillwater needs a restart for changes to take effect.
settings.base_path.restart_required_body: Restart Stillwater for the new base path to take effect. The HTTP routes are bound at startup and cannot be re-bound on the fly.
settings.base_path.restart_required_titl: Restart required to apply the new base path
settings.base_path.title: Base Path
settings.behavior.description: These switches set the instance-wide defaults Stillwater applies during metadata workflows, such as whether NFO writes use symlinks for duplicate filenames. They affect every artist on this instance, not just the one you are looking at.
settings.behavior.help: Defaults applied when Stillwater fetches and writes metadata: which fields auto-fill, which require confirmation, and how aggressively existing values are overwritten.
settings.behavior.title: Behavior
settings.confirm_dialogs.description: Destructive actions in Stillwater (delete an artist, clear a cache, revoke a token) prompt you to confirm before going through. Each dialog has a Don't ask again checkbox; this section lists every dialog you have suppressed so you can re-enable the prompt if you change your mind.
settings.confirm_dialogs.help: Some destructive actions in Stillwater open a confirmation dialog before proceeding (deleting an artist, restoring provider defaults, revoking an API token). These toggles let you suppress dialogs for actions you've grown comfortable with; turn them back on anytime.
settings.confirm_dialogs.reset_info: If you previously checked "Don't ask again" on a confirmation dialog, you can reset all preferences here to restore those prompts.
settings.confirm_dialogs.title: Confirmation Dialogs
settings.connections.api_key.help: The API key Stillwater uses to authenticate requests to this server. Generate or copy it from the upstream server's API Keys admin section. Stillwater stores it encrypted at rest.
settings.connections.base_url.help: The base URL of the media server, including protocol and port (for example, http://192.168.1.100:8096). Stillwater uses this to reach the server's API. Use an address that is accessible from wherever Stillwater is running.
settings.connections.description: A connection is a credentialed link to an external media server (Emby, Jellyfin, or Lidarr) that lets Stillwater both read its library structure and write NFO files and images back into the artist folders that server manages. Add one connection per server you want Stillwater to integrate with.
settings.connections.feature_image_write: When on, Stillwater downloads images from providers and writes them to artist folders that this server's libraries cover. Writes can still be paused by the conflict banner shown at the top of the page when a round-trip with the platform's own image saver would otherwise overwrite Stillwater's edits.

When on, Stillwater writes image files for artists in this server's libraries. Writes can still be gated while conflict gating is active (write-back or round-trip overlap) -- see the top banner for details.
settings.connections.feature_library_imp: When on, Stillwater imports library and artist metadata from this server during scans.
When on, Stillwater imports library metadata from this server.
settings.connections.feature_nfo_write.d: When on, Stillwater writes artist.nfo files into folders this server's libraries cover. Writes can still be paused by the conflict banner shown at the top of the page when a round-trip with the platform's own NFO saver would otherwise overwrite Stillwater's edits.
settings.connections.feature_nfo_write_t: When on, Stillwater writes artist.nfo files for artists in this server's libraries. Writes can still be gated while conflict gating is active (write-back or round-trip overlap) -- see the top banner for details.
settings.connections.help: Each connection links Stillwater to one media server. Connections drive library imports, NFO writes, and image writes; per-server feature toggles let you opt in or out of each behavior. Stillwater encrypts API keys at rest.
settings.connections.manage_description: When on, Stillwater watches this server and turns off its images and NFO savers whenever they get re-enabled. Your previous settings are saved and restored if you turn this off or remove the connection.
settings.connections.manage_error: Could not update this server-managed setting. Try again or reload the page.
settings.connections.manage_title: Let Stillwater manage images and NFO files on this server
settings.connections.manage_title.help: When on, Stillwater actively suppresses the media server's built-in images and NFO writers so they do not overwrite the files Stillwater maintains. Turn this off if you want Stillwater and the media server to coexist without interference.
settings.connections.sends_heading: What Stillwater sends to this connection
settings.connections.title: Server Connections
settings.connections.verify_path_descrip: Catches the rare case where Lidarr silently coerces the submitted path against the Root Folder list. Adds one GET per rename.
settings.connections.verify_path_error: Could not update the verify-path setting. Try again or reload the page.
settings.db_maintenance.auto_schedule.de: How often Stillwater runs the optimize task in the background. The schedule applies after a restart.
settings.db_maintenance.auto_schedule.he: How often the background optimize task runs automatically. Changes take effect after a restart. More frequent schedules keep query performance stable but add a small overhead on each run.
settings.db_maintenance.confirm_vacuum: VACUUM rebuilds the entire database file. This may take a while for large databases and temporarily doubles disk usage. Continue?
settings.db_maintenance.description: SQLite databases accumulate fragmentation over time, slowing queries and leaving freed pages on disk. Maintenance runs VACUUM and ANALYZE to compact the database file and refresh query planner statistics; trigger it manually or schedule it to run on its own.
settings.db_maintenance.help: SQLite reclaims space and rebuilds indexes through PRAGMA optimize and VACUUM. Stillwater can run these on a schedule so query performance stays stable as the database grows; manual triggers are also available for one-off maintenance.
settings.db_maintenance.schedule_note: Runs PRAGMA optimize and WAL checkpoint on the selected interval. Requires restart to apply schedule changes.
settings.db_maintenance.title: Database Maintenance
settings.description: Settings cover everything global to this Stillwater instance: which media platform you target, which metadata sources feed it, and how its output is shaped on disk. Most users only need to revisit this page when adding a new connection or rotating an API key.
settings.export_import.description_line1: Export all settings (provider keys, connections, profiles, webhooks) as an encrypted file.
settings.export_import.description_line2: A passphrase you choose protects the file, so it can be imported on any Stillwater instance.
settings.export_import.encryption_note_l: The export file is encrypted with your passphrase using PBKDF2 + AES-256-GCM.
You will need the same passphrase to import the file on any instance.
settings.export_import.export_passphrase: Pick a passphrase used to encrypt the exported file. You will need the same passphrase to import the file later.
Pick a passphrase used to encrypt the exported file. You will need the same passphrase to import the file later.
settings.export_import.help: Settings export bundles your provider keys, connections, profiles, libraries, webhooks, and API tokens into a single encrypted file you choose a passphrase for. Use it to back up your configuration off-host or to clone a working setup onto a fresh Stillwater instance.
settings.export_import.import_file_label: Pick the encrypted .json file produced by a previous export.
Pick the encrypted .json file produced by a previous export.
settings.export_import.import_passphrase: Enter the same passphrase used when the file was exported.
Enter the same passphrase used when the file was exported.
Enter the passphrase used during export
settings.export_import.title: Settings Export / Import
settings.image_cache.clear.description: Remove every image currently held in the local cache. Cached images are re-fetched from providers the next time an artist screen is opened.
settings.image_cache.clear.help: Removes all locally cached images immediately. Artists are still visible but their images are re-fetched from providers on next access.
settings.image_cache.description: When an artist comes from a connected media server but Stillwater cannot resolve the artist's folder on disk, downloaded cover art is stored in a local image cache instead so the UI can still render thumbnails. This section shows the cache size, lets you cap how big it can grow, and lets you clear it.
settings.image_cache.help: Stillwater caches downloaded images locally. Set a maximum size to keep disk usage bounded; clearing the cache removes all downloaded images and they will be re-fetched on next use.
settings.image_cache.max_size.descriptio: Cap the total disk space the image cache may use. When the cache reaches this size, the oldest entries are evicted first. Pick from 256 MB, 512 MB, 1 GB, 2 GB, or Unlimited.
settings.image_cache.max_size.help: Limits how much disk space cached images may occupy. When the cache reaches this size the oldest entries are evicted first. Choose Unlimited to never evict automatically.
settings.image_cache.title: Image Cache
settings.image_cache.unlimited.descripti: When the maximum size is set to Unlimited, Stillwater never evicts cached images automatically. Disk usage grows with each new image fetched.
settings.libraries.description: A library is a top-level directory containing one folder per artist; that is the layout Emby, Jellyfin, and Kodi all expect. Add a library entry for each such directory you want Stillwater to scan and write into. Filesystem watch mode is configured per entry below.
settings.libraries.fs_both.description: Combine native filesystem events with periodic polling. Useful when watching alone misses some changes (for example, on certain network shares).
settings.libraries.fs_mode_title.descrip: How Stillwater detects new or changed files in this library. Watching subscribes to native filesystem events; polling re-scans on a fixed interval.
settings.libraries.fs_mode_title.help: How Stillwater detects new or changed files in this library. Watching subscribes to native filesystem events; polling re-scans on a fixed interval.
settings.libraries.fs_off.description: Stillwater does not monitor this library's filesystem. New files are picked up only by manual scans.
settings.libraries.fs_poll.description: Periodically re-scan the library for changes. Works on every filesystem but adds some delay between a change and Stillwater noticing it.
settings.libraries.fs_watch.description: Subscribe to native filesystem events so changes are picked up immediately. Recommended for local disks.
settings.libraries.help: A library is a directory full of artist folders that Stillwater scans, watches, and writes NFO/image files into. Add one library per top-level music root. Per-library settings control how Stillwater detects new files and whether NFO writes are locked for that library.
settings.libraries.lock_nfo_label.descri: When on, Stillwater stamps a lockdata flag into every NFO it writes for this library. This tells Emby and Jellyfin to refuse metadata refreshes for those artists so Stillwater's curated values are not overwritten by the platform's own scrapers. Off by default. Artists whose NFO already contains a lockdata flag (set by Stillwater or another tool) are automatically marked as locked at the artist level.
settings.libraries.lock_nfo_label.help: When on, Stillwater stamps a lockdata flag into every NFO it writes for this library. This tells Emby and Jellyfin to refuse metadata refreshes for those artists so Stillwater's curated values are not overwritten by the platform's own scrapers. Off by default. Artists whose NFO already contains a lockdata flag (set by Stillwater or another tool) are automatically marked as locked at the artist level.
settings.libraries.name: Library Name
settings.libraries.name.help: A short label to identify this library in the UI. It does not have to match the folder name on disk.
settings.libraries.path.help: The absolute path to the root directory containing one folder per artist. Stillwater scans this directory and writes NFO and image files into it. The path must be accessible from the server where Stillwater is running.
settings.libraries.poll_interval_title.h: How often Stillwater re-scans the library directory when poll mode is active. Shorter intervals detect changes faster at the cost of more frequent disk reads.
settings.libraries.title: Music Libraries
settings.libraries.type_regular.descript: Standard music library where the artist folder maps to the performing artist.
settings.log_settings.description: Stillwater emits structured log lines for every meaningful event (scans, rule fixes, API calls, errors). These settings decide how chatty those logs are, what format they use, and whether they are kept on disk in addition to stdout. Changes take effect immediately.
settings.log_settings.file_path.descript: Where Stillwater writes the rotating log file. Use a path inside your config directory so the log persists across container restarts.
settings.log_settings.file_path.help: Where the rotating log file is written. Use a path inside your persistent config directory so logs survive container restarts.
settings.log_settings.file_path_note: Logs are always written to stdout. This enables an additional rotating file.
settings.log_settings.files_to_keep.desc: Number of rotated log files to retain alongside the active one. Older files are removed during rotation.
settings.log_settings.files_to_keep.help: How many rotated log files to keep on disk alongside the active log. Older archives are removed during rotation once this count is exceeded.
settings.log_settings.format.description: JSON is easier to ingest into log shippers and search tools. Text is more readable when you are reading the file directly.
settings.log_settings.format.help: JSON output is structured and easy to ingest into log shippers or search tools. Text is human-readable plain lines, better for direct inspection.
settings.log_settings.help: Stillwater writes a structured log of everything it does -- scans, rule fixes, API calls, errors. These controls govern where logs are written, what severity is captured, and how aggressively they're rotated to keep disk usage bounded.
settings.log_settings.level.description: Minimum severity to record. Trace and Debug are verbose and intended for troubleshooting; Info is a good default for normal operation.
settings.log_settings.level.help: Minimum severity to emit. Info is the recommended default. Debug and Trace are verbose and intended for diagnosing specific issues; avoid leaving them on in production.
settings.log_settings.log_to_file.descri: Write logs to a rotating file in addition to stdout.
settings.log_settings.log_to_file.help: When on, Stillwater writes a rotating log file in addition to stdout. Configure the path, size limit, and rotation policy in the fields below.
settings.log_settings.log_to_file.label: Log to file
settings.log_settings.max_age.descriptio: Discard rotated log files older than this many days, regardless of how many files exist.
settings.log_settings.max_age.help: Rotated log files older than this many days are removed, regardless of the files-to-keep count. Both limits apply independently.
settings.log_settings.max_size.descripti: Rotate the log file once it grows past this size. The active file becomes a numbered archive and a new active file is started.
settings.log_settings.max_size.help: The active log file is rotated once it reaches this size. The old file becomes a numbered archive and a new file is started.
Apply the new log level only for the current process. The persisted level is restored when Stillwater restarts. Useful for temporary debug sessions.
settings.log_settings.title: Log Settings
settings.log_viewer.description: Stillwater keeps the most recent log lines in an in-memory ring buffer in addition to writing them to disk. The viewer streams that buffer live so you can watch what the app is doing right now, filter by severity, and grep across messages without leaving the browser.
settings.log_viewer.footer_note: Showing up to 200 most recent entries. Live view polls in real time; historical files are loaded on demand.
settings.log_viewer.help: Live tail of the most recent Stillwater log entries with filter controls. Use it to watch what's happening during a scan, troubleshoot a misbehaving provider, or capture a snippet for a bug report without shelling into the host.
settings.log_viewer.title: Log Viewer
settings.metadata_languages.default_hint: No languages set. Using default: English. Search above to add languages.
settings.metadata_languages.description: Set your preferred languages for artist names, biographies, aliases, and genre/style/mood tags. Search by language name and pick from the autocomplete to add pills. Order matters: pills are an ordered priority list, left to right. When a provider has the same field in multiple languages, Stillwater walks the list and uses the first language the provider offers; everything to the right is a fallback. Drag pills or focus one and use the arrow keys to reorder; click the X or press Backspace on a focused pill to remove. Clearing all pills falls back to English silently.
settings.metadata_languages.help: Controls which language metadata providers return for artist names, biographies, and other text. The first language in the list takes priority. If a provider has no data in your preferred language, it falls back to the next one.
settings.metadata_languages.title: Metadata Language Preferences
settings.metadata_languages.romanization: When your top metadata language is Latin-script (English, German, French, etc.) and MusicBrainz does not have a tagged alias in that language, Stillwater can use MB's sort-name as the display name (e.g. 青木達之 becomes Tatsuyuki Aoki). Disable this if you want to see only the canonical name or curator-tagged aliases.
Use MB sort-name as a fallback for display names
Failed to save romanization fallback preference
settings.name_similarity.description: Minimum similarity score (0-100) required when matching artist names from search results. Set to 0 to disable name validation and accept any result. Default is 60.
settings.name_similarity.help: Controls how closely an artist name from a provider must match the local name. Lower values accept fuzzier matches (useful for transliterated names). Higher values require near-exact matches (fewer false positives).
settings.name_similarity.label: Name Similarity Threshold
settings.next.subtitle: Everything here is searchable. Filter the rail or jump to a section.
Each violation has a severity (info, warning, error). These toggles decide which severities the sidebar badge tallies; disabling a severity hides it from the count without removing those violations from the Reports page itself.
Choose which violation severities contribute to the badge count. Unchecking a severity hides it from the count but does not delete the underlying violations.
settings.notif_badges.description: A violation is what Stillwater records when an enabled rule disagrees with what the artist's NFO or images contain on disk. The sidebar badge surfaces the count of active violations directly in the navigation so you do not have to open the Reports page to know whether anything needs attention.
settings.notif_badges.enable_badge.descr: When on, the Reports link in the sidebar carries a small numeric badge whose count is the number of active violations matching the severity filters below. Turn it off if you would rather not see the count at a glance.
settings.notif_badges.enable_badge.help: Shows a violation count on the Reports sidebar link. Turn off to hide the badge while still retaining all violations on the Reports page.
settings.notif_badges.help: Controls the violation count badge shown on the Reports link in the sidebar. Enable badge and the per-severity filters control which violations contribute to the numeric counter.
settings.notif_badges.title: Notification Badges
settings.operations.description: Operational tuning knobs for scanning and rule evaluation. These were previously available only as environment variables.
settings.operations.env_managed: Set via the %s environment variable. Edit that variable and restart to change it.
settings.operations.exclusions.descripti: Comma-separated artist directory names the scanner skips (for example: Various Artists, Soundtrack). Whitespace around each name is trimmed. Takes effect on the next scan.
settings.operations.exclusions.label: Scanner Exclusions
settings.operations.exclusions.placehold: Various Artists, Various, VA, Soundtrack, OST
settings.operations.help: Advanced scanning and rule-engine settings. The scanner settings take effect on the next scan; the artist-worker count applies on the next rule pass.
settings.operations.mtime.description: When on, the scanner reuses cached image flags for artist directories whose modification time has not changed since the previous scan, skipping a re-probe. Turn off on filesystems with unreliable modification times (some network shares, FUSE mounts, restored backups).
settings.operations.mtime.label: Scanner mtime Fast Path
settings.operations.title: Performance & Scanning
settings.operations.workers.caution_body: Raising this only helps if your metadata providers permit higher request throughput, such as a self-hosted or mirrored MusicBrainz. The shared per-provider rate limiter still caps requests to the public providers, so on a default install a high value mostly adds database write contention with no speedup. Most deployments should leave this at the default.
settings.operations.workers.caution_titl: Advanced: only raise this for self-hosted or mirrored providers
settings.operations.workers.description: How many artists the rule engine evaluates concurrently during a Run Rules pass. Default is 2. Applies on the next pass.
settings.operations.workers.label: Concurrent Artists
settings.onboarding_section_body: Restart the first-run wizard. Your settings -- libraries, providers, connections, profile -- are preserved.
settings.rerun_confirm_body: This restarts the first-run wizard from step 1. Your settings will be preserved.
settings.platform_debug.description: Each artist who came in from a connected media server has a raw payload Stillwater received from the platform's API (the source of truth for IDs, image URLs, and library paths). Turning this on adds a Debug tab to the artist detail page that displays that payload, which is useful when tracing why a field looks wrong.
settings.platform_debug.help: When on, a Debug tab appears on each artist's detail page showing the raw API payload received from the connected media server. Useful for diagnosing why an ID, image URL, or library path looks unexpected.
settings.platform_debug.title: Show platform debug info on artist pages
settings.platform_debug.toast_save_faile: Failed to update platform debug setting.
settings.platform_profile.description: A platform profile bundles the NFO format and image filename conventions for one media platform (Emby, Jellyfin, or Kodi), since each platform expects metadata laid out differently on disk. The active profile is the one Stillwater uses when writing files; change it here to retarget output for a different platform.
settings.platform_profile.help: Profiles bundle the NFO format and image filename conventions for a specific media platform (Emby, Jellyfin, Kodi). Picking a profile here is what tells Stillwater how to write files so your platform reads them.
settings.platform_profile.title: Platform Profile
settings.priorities.confirm_restore: Restore default provider priorities? This re-enables every provider and resets the order for every field.
settings.priorities.description: For each metadata field (biography, genres, image URLs, and so on) Stillwater queries providers in a specific order and uses the first non-empty answer. This list is that order: drag to rearrange, click the checkmark or X to include or skip a provider for a given field. Only providers you have configured appear here.
settings.priorities.help: When multiple providers return data for the same field, Stillwater uses the first enabled provider in priority order. Drag to reorder. Disabled providers are skipped during metadata fetching.
settings.priorities.instructions: Drag to reorder. Click to enable/disable.
settings.priorities.no_providers: No configured providers for this field.
settings.priorities.title: Provider Priorities
settings.profile_naming.error_no_extensi: Filename must have an extension (.jpg, .jpeg or .png).
settings.profile_naming.error_path_separ: Filename must not contain path separators.
settings.provider_config.beta.descriptio: Send requests to the MusicBrainz beta server. The data set matches the official server, but the deployment runs preview code. Subject to the same one-request-per-second public rate limit.
settings.provider_config.client_id.descr: The OAuth application identifier issued by the provider when you registered an app. Paste it here so Stillwater can authenticate against the provider's API.
settings.provider_config.client_secret.d: The OAuth application secret issued alongside the Client ID. Stored encrypted at rest. Treat it like a password and do not share it.
settings.provider_config.custom_help: Self-hosted mirrors can often handle higher rates. Default: 10 req/s.
settings.provider_config.custom_mirror.d: Point Stillwater at a self-hosted MusicBrainz mirror. Requests bypass the public rate limit so you can raise throughput, subject to whatever your mirror can sustain. If the mirror returns an invalid response (for example an HTML error page instead of JSON), Stillwater logs a warning and automatically retries that request against the official MusicBrainz API, so a misconfigured mirror degrades gracefully instead of silently losing metadata.
settings.provider_config.oauth_credentia: Sub-section for OAuth application credentials used when a provider requires authenticated access. Fill in the Client ID and Client Secret issued by the provider.
settings.provider_config.oauth_note: Required for submitting edits to MusicBrainz.
settings.provider_config.official.descri: Send requests to musicbrainz.org. The public service is rate-limited to roughly one request per second.
settings.provider_config.server.descript: Which MusicBrainz endpoint Stillwater queries. Pick a preset or supply a custom mirror URL.
settings.provider_config.verbosity_biogr: Controls how much of the Wikipedia article is fetched for the biography field.
Fetch the complete Wikipedia article text for the biography field. The result may be lengthy for well-documented artists.
settings.provider_config.verbosity_intro: Fetch only the introductory lead section from the Wikipedia article. This is the default and produces a concise biography.
settings.provider_config.verbosity_secti: Controls the level of detail fetched from the provider for specific metadata fields.
settings.provider_keys.description: Most metadata providers (Fanart.tv, Discogs, TheAudioDB, and others) require a free or paid API key before they will return data. Paste each key here to enable that provider; keys are encrypted at rest. Providers without a key are skipped during metadata lookups.
settings.provider_keys.free_tier.descrip: Shown next to a provider that works without a key but offers an optional premium key for higher rate limits or extra features. Add a key here to upgrade.
settings.provider_keys.help: Some metadata providers require an API key to query. Add the key issued by the provider here so Stillwater can include them in lookups; providers without a key are skipped silently. Keys are stored encrypted at rest.
settings.provider_keys.key_configured.de: Shown next to a provider that requires an API key once a valid key has been saved. Stillwater will include this provider in metadata lookups.
settings.provider_keys.key_required.desc: Shown next to a provider that needs an API key but does not have one saved yet. Stillwater skips this provider during lookups until a key is added.
settings.provider_keys.no_key_required.d: Shown next to a provider that works without any API key. Nothing to configure here.
settings.provider_keys.premium_configure: Shown next to a provider with optional premium tier when a premium key has been saved. The provider is queried using the upgraded access.
settings.provider_keys.spotify_note_pref: Requires a Spotify Premium subscription and a registered
settings.provider_keys.title: Provider API Keys
settings.rule_schedule.description: A rule is a check that compares the actual state of an artist's NFO or images against the value Stillwater believes is correct.
