Scheduling makes Stillwater run every enabled rule across the whole library on a fixed cadence (in addition to triggering them on changes). Requires a container restart after changing.
settings.rule_schedule.help: Periodically evaluates all enabled rules across your library. Rules set to auto-fix correct violations automatically; rules set to manual report violations without changing files.
settings.rule_schedule.note: Evaluates all enabled rules against every artist on the selected interval. Changes take effect after container restart.
settings.rule_schedule.title: Scheduled Evaluation
settings.rules.auto_fix.description: When this rule is enabled, the dropdown picks how Stillwater handles a failed check: manual records the violation on the Reports page so you can review and apply fixes yourself, while auto applies the fix during the same scan without waiting for review. Disabling the rule is a separate toggle.
settings.rules.auto_fix.help: Sets whether violations are fixed automatically during scans or queued for manual review on the Reports page. Auto applies corrections without confirmation; Manual records them so you can review and apply individually.
settings.rules.cannot_enable_tooltip: Cannot enable: no local library configured
settings.rules.catalogue_link_aria: Read more about this rule in the catalogue
settings.rules.conflict_gated_chip.descr: A conflict gate is Stillwater's safeguard that pauses its own writes when a connected media server has its own NFO or image saver enabled, since whichever wrote second wins and edits would otherwise round-trip endlessly. This chip appears on the NFO or Image rule category header while a gate is active for that surface; auto-fix stays paused until you turn off the platform-side saver and dismiss the banner.
settings.rules.conflict_gated_image_tool: Image-category rules are paused while conflict gating is active. Resolve the active write-back or round-trip conflict in the top banner to resume auto-fix.
settings.rules.conflict_gated_nfo_toolti: NFO-category rules are paused while conflict gating is active. Resolve the active write-back or round-trip conflict in the top banner to resume auto-fix.
settings.rules.help_image: Image rules check for missing, oversized, or incorrectly formatted artist images (posters, banners, logos). Auto-fix mode can fetch and resize images automatically.
settings.rules.help_metadata: Metadata rules verify artist data quality (genres, biographies, sort names, etc.). Auto-fix mode fetches corrections from configured providers.
settings.rules.help_conflict_gated_image: The "paused: conflict gating" chip means image-category rule fixes are blocked because a connected media server has its own image saver on, two connections share a library path, or Stillwater could not confirm a peer's state. The pause prevents write-back loops and round-trips. To resume, disable the server's image saver or use the "Let Stillwater manage" toggle on the connection.
settings.rules.help_conflict_gated_nfo: The "paused: conflict gating" chip means NFO-category rule fixes are blocked because a connected media server has its own NFO saver on, two connections share a library path, or Stillwater could not confirm a peer's state. The pause prevents write-back loops and round-trips. To resume, disable the server's NFO saver or use the "Let Stillwater manage" toggle on the connection.
settings.rules.help_requires_local_chip: The "Requires local library" chip marks a rule that must read or write files on disk. These rules are unavailable when no library has a configured filesystem path. Add at least one library with a real directory path to enable them.
settings.rules.help_severity_badge: Severity shows how strongly the rule flags a violation: info is informational, warning flags issues worth reviewing, error flags the most serious problems. Severity is set per rule and cannot be changed in the UI; fixers and the conflict gate run identically across all three levels.
settings.rules.help_nfo: NFO rules validate and fix artist information files. Each rule can be disabled, set to manual (notify only), or auto-fix (automatically correct violations during scans).
settings.rules.manual.description: When this rule is enabled, the dropdown picks how Stillwater handles a failed check: manual records the violation on the Reports page so you can review and apply fixes yourself, while auto applies the fix during the same scan. Disabling the rule is a separate toggle.
settings.rules.requires_local.descriptio: Some rules check or rewrite files on disk, which only works for libraries whose path Stillwater can read directly (not API-only libraries imported through Emby or Jellyfin). This badge marks a rule that depends on local-filesystem access; the rule stays disabled until you add at least one library with a real filesystem path on the Libraries tab.
settings.rules.requires_local_tooltip: This rule requires a local library with a filesystem path. Add a library with a path to enable it.
settings.rules.requires_local_tooltip_sh: Requires a local library with a filesystem path
settings.symlinks.description: When the platform profile maps several filenames to the same image, write the first as a real file and create symlinks for the rest. Saves disk space without breaking platforms that expect every filename to exist.
settings.symlinks.help: When a platform profile maps multiple filenames to the same image type, Stillwater writes one real file and creates symbolic links for the others. Saves disk space without confusing platforms that expect each filename to exist.
settings.symlinks.supported_description: When multiple filenames are configured for an image type, create symlinks instead of byte-for-byte copies. %s images are always real files.
settings.symlinks.title: Use symlinks for duplicates
settings.symlinks.unsupported_descriptio: Symlinks are not supported on this filesystem. The library path does not support symbolic links.
settings.tab.logs.intro: The Logs tab is divided into two sections. Log Settings controls what Stillwater captures: the minimum severity level, the on-disk format, and the rotation policy that determines how many files are kept and for how long. Log Viewer reads back what Log Settings retained, streaming the in-memory ring buffer live and letting you page through older rotated files; it can only show entries that the retention limits above have not already pruned.
settings.tab.maintenance.intro: The Maintenance tab groups five sections that keep the database and configuration healthy. Database Maintenance and Database Backup each have an auto-run option; the Schedule section supplies the shared interval choices (every 6 hours through weekly) that both use. Confirmation Dialogs lists every destructive-action prompt you have suppressed with Don't ask again, so you can re-enable any of them. Settings Export / Import lets you snapshot your full configuration as an encrypted file and restore it on the same or a different instance.
settings.tag_sources.caps.description: Set to 0 to allow an unlimited number of tags. When a cap is set, the highest-priority provider's tags fill the slots first.
settings.tag_sources.caps.label: Tag Count Caps
settings.tag_sources.description: Filter genre, style, and mood tags written to artist metadata. Exclude patterns drop matching tags before they are saved; count caps limit how many tags of each type are kept.
settings.tag_sources.exclude.description: One pattern per line, case-insensitive. An asterisk (*) is a wildcard that matches any run of characters. Blank lines are ignored.
settings.tag_sources.exclude.help: Each non-blank line is a pattern matched case-insensitively against genre, style, and mood tags. The asterisk (*) is a wildcard matching any run of characters. Matched tags are dropped before the NFO is written.
settings.tag_sources.exclude.label: Exclude Patterns
settings.tag_sources.help: Controls which genre, style, and mood tags reach your NFO files. Exclude patterns drop any tag matching the pattern (case-insensitive; * is a wildcard). Count caps limit the total number of tags written per field. Both filters run after provider merging, so they apply regardless of which provider supplied the tags.
settings.tag_sources.title: Tag Sources
settings.title: Settings
settings.tls_status.acme_experimental_ba: Small chip rendered next to the Active (ACME) status when ACME auto-cert is in use. Signals that the ACME path (Let's Encrypt today; future providers such as ZeroSSL) is unverified and should be treated as preview-only until validated against a real deployment.
settings.tls_status.acme_experimental_no: ACME auto-cert is unverified by the project today. Treat it as a preview and prefer bring-your-own certificates (SW_TLS_CERT_FILE / SW_TLS_KEY_FILE) for production deployments.
Amber caveat paragraph shown below the status line when ACME is active. Sets operator expectations until the ACME path has been validated end-to-end against a public deployment.
settings.tls_status.active_acme_with_dom: Status shown when ACME auto-cert is active and SW_ACME_DOMAIN is set. The domain name is substituted into the display label at runtime.
settings.tls_status.description: Read-only summary of the HTTPS listener. Configure TLS via SW_TLS_CERT_FILE / SW_TLS_KEY_FILE for a bring-your-own certificate, or SW_ACME_DOMAIN for an automatic ACME-managed certificate (Let's Encrypt by default). Enable HTTP/3 (QUIC) with SW_HTTP3_ENABLED=true.
settings.tls_status.help: Stillwater can serve over HTTPS using either a certificate you provide (SW_TLS_CERT_FILE / SW_TLS_KEY_FILE or [server.tls] in config.toml) or an automatic ACME-managed certificate (Let's Encrypt by default; alternate directories supported via SW_ACME_CA). The optional HTTP redirect listener (SW_HTTP_REDIRECT_PORT, typically 80) appears here when active and 301-redirects every plain-HTTP request to the HTTPS port. HTTP/3 (QUIC) layers on top of TLS to give modern clients lower-latency connections over UDP; clients with UDP blocked fall back to HTTP/1.1 or HTTP/2 over TCP automatically. This card shows which mode is active and which ports are bound; configuration is read-only here.
settings.tls_status.listener_http.descri: Plain-HTTP listener. The port number is substituted at runtime.
settings.tls_status.listener_http3.descr: HTTP/3 (QUIC) listener over UDP. The port number is substituted at runtime.
settings.tls_status.listener_https.descr: HTTPS listener. The port number is substituted at runtime.
settings.tls_status.listener_redirect.de: HTTP redirect listener: every plain-HTTP request on this port receives a 301 redirect to the HTTPS port.
settings.tls_status.listener_redirect.vi: Only present when SW_HTTP_REDIRECT_PORT is configured.
settings.tls_status.title: TLS Status
settings.updates.auto_check.description: When on, a background task polls GitHub at the configured interval and notes whether a newer release is available, surfacing a banner in the UI when one appears. Checking is read-only; it never installs anything on its own.
settings.updates.auto_check.help: Periodically checks GitHub for newer releases and shows a banner when one is available. Checking is read-only; it never installs anything on its own.
settings.updates.auto_check.label: Automatic update checks
settings.updates.auto_update.confirm_bod: This will install future releases without manual review. Rollback via the GitHub Releases page.
settings.updates.auto_update.description: When on, Stillwater installs new releases automatically as they become available. This setting has no effect on Docker installs; Docker users update by pulling a new image and recreating the container.
settings.updates.auto_update.help: When on, new releases matching the selected channel are installed automatically without manual review. No effect on Docker installs. You can roll back via the GitHub Releases page.
settings.updates.auto_update.label: Automatically install updates
settings.updates.channel.description: Pick which kinds of releases to receive. Stable is the recommended default and only delivers fully tested versions. Prerelease also includes release candidates that are still being polished. Nightly delivers in-progress builds from active development; expect rough edges.
settings.updates.channel.help: Stable delivers fully tested releases only. Prerelease includes release candidates. Nightly provides in-progress builds; expect rough edges.
settings.updates.channel.label: Release channel
settings.updates.check_interval.descript: How often the background task polls the GitHub releases API to look for newer builds. Shorter intervals find releases sooner; longer intervals are gentler on GitHub's rate limit. The minimum is once per hour.
settings.updates.check_interval.label: Check interval
settings.updates.config.description: These settings govern the auto-update workflow: which release channel Stillwater watches (stable, prerelease, or nightly), how often it polls GitHub, and whether it installs new builds automatically or just notifies you.
settings.updates.config.help: Update channel and check schedule. Channel picks how bleeding-edge the releases you receive are; the schedule controls how often Stillwater polls GitHub for new ones. Auto-install is configured below in this card.
settings.updates.config.label: Update channel and schedule
settings.updates.description: Stillwater can update its own binary in place by downloading the latest release from GitHub and swapping the executable on next restart. Settings here decide whether to check, how often, and which release channel to follow. (Docker installs ignore these settings; update by pulling a new image instead.)
settings.updates.docker_notice: Binary updates are not available in Docker deployments. To upgrade, re-pull the container image with the desired tag (e.g. docker pull ghcr.io/sydlexius/stillwater:latest) and restart your container.
settings.updates.enabled.description: The master switch for everything in this section: when off, Stillwater never polls GitHub, never shows update banners, and the Apply Update button is grayed out regardless of the channel and auto-check settings below. Turn it off in environments where the binary is managed by an external system.
settings.updates.enabled.help: Master switch for the entire updater. When off, Stillwater never polls GitHub, shows no update banners, and ignores the auto-check and auto-install settings below.
settings.updates.enabled.label: Updater enabled
settings.updates.help: Stillwater checks GitHub Releases for new versions and (on non-Docker hosts) can install them in place. The schedule, channel, and apply behavior are all controllable; on Docker, updates are documented but applied through the container image rather than this UI.
settings.updates.last_auto_applied.label: Last auto-applied
settings.updates.load_failed_body: Reload the page before changing any value. Saving now would overwrite your settings with defaults.
settings.updates.load_failed_save_blocke: Save blocked: configuration failed to load. Reload the page.
settings.updates.restart_required_body: The new binary is in place; restart Stillwater to finish updating.
settings.updates.restart_required_docker: Recreate the container to load the new binary (e.g. docker compose up -d --force-recreate stillwater).
settings.updates.restart_required_native: Stop and re-run the Stillwater binary (or use your process manager: systemctl restart stillwater).
settings.updates.skip_version.label: Skip %s
settings.updates.title: Application Updates
settings.users.delete_dialog_irreversibl: This permanently removes the account and cannot be undone.
settings.users.delete_dialog_reason_labe: Reason (optional, recorded in the audit log)
settings.users.delete_dialog_reason_plac: e.g. left team, never redeemed invite
settings.users.delete_protected_tooltip: The bootstrap administrator account cannot be deleted.
settings.users.delete_self_tooltip: Use Account Settings to delete your own account.
settings.users.description: User accounts and pending invites both live here. An account is someone who can already sign in; an invite is a single-use link that creates an account when the recipient redeems it. Use this tab to issue invites, change roles, and revoke access.
settings.users.enable_multi_user.help: When off, Stillwater runs in single-user mode and assumes one administrator owns the instance. Turning this on unlocks invite links, per-account roles, and separate preferences for each signed-in user.
settings.users.enable_multi_user.label: Multi-User Mode
settings.users.expires_in.description: How long the new invite link remains usable. After this period the link expires and can no longer redeem an account.
settings.users.expires_in.help: How long the invite link stays valid after it is generated. Choose a shorter window when the recipient should sign up promptly; use a longer window for low-urgency onboarding. The link cannot be redeemed after it expires.
settings.users.expires_in.label: Invite Expiry
settings.users.expires_label.description: Marks the expiry timestamp shown next to a pending invite in the list below. The invite stops working after this time.
settings.users.help: Multi-user mode lets multiple people share one Stillwater instance with their own accounts and roles instead of one shared login. Invites generate one-time signup links you can hand out; the user table below shows everyone who has signed in and what role they hold.
settings.users.inactive_only_help: Filters the table to accounts that have never logged in or have not signed in for the configured staleness window. Bulk delete is only available while this filter is on, so the live team cannot be selected by accident. Configure the day threshold via the users.inactive_threshold_days setting (default 90).
settings.users.invite_expiry.description: Accessible label for the expiry-duration selector when creating an invite. Mirrors the visible Expires In control.
settings.users.link_single_use: This link can only be used once.
settings.users.multi_user_mode.descripti: Single-user mode is the default and assumes one administrator owns the whole instance; multi-user mode unlocks invites, per-user roles, and per-account preferences so several people can share the same Stillwater. Turn it on before you create the first invite for someone other than yourself.
settings.users.multi_user_mode.label: Multi-User Mode
settings.users.pending_invites.descripti: An invite is a one-time link Stillwater issues to a prospective user; redeeming it creates an account at the role baked into the link. This list shows invites that have been issued but not yet redeemed, so you can revoke or copy each link before it expires.
settings.users.pending_invites.help: Invites are time-limited single-use signup links you generate above. This list shows invites that have been issued but not yet redeemed; revoke any link you sent in error to invalidate it before someone clicks it.
settings.users.pending_invites.label: Pending Invites
settings.users.revoke_confirm: Revoke this invite? It will no longer be usable.
settings.users.role.description: The permission level granted to the new account when this invite is redeemed. Admins manage settings; standard users browse and tag their own library.
settings.users.role_for_invite.descripti: Accessible label for the role selector when creating an invite. Mirrors the visible Role control.
settings.users.role_for_invite.help: The permission level the new account will have when this invite is redeemed. Administrators can change settings and manage other users; Operators can tag and browse the library but cannot change system configuration.
settings.users.role_for_invite.label: Invite Role
settings.users.role_label.description: Marks the role badge shown next to a pending invite in the list below. The redeemed account will be created with this role.
settings.users.title: Users
settings.users.user_accounts.description: An account is anyone who has signed in successfully and exists in Stillwater's user table, regardless of which auth provider verified them. This table lists every active account; use the row controls to promote or demote a user's role or deactivate them.
settings.web_search.description: Authoritative sources like Fanart.tv and TheAudioDB curate a fixed catalogue of images per artist; for obscure or local artists they often have nothing. Web image search (DuckDuckGo) crawls the open web for matches, giving Stillwater far more candidates at the cost of mixed quality. No API key required.
settings.web_search.help: Falls back to general-purpose web image search when authoritative providers (MusicBrainz, Fanart.tv, etc.) don't return an image. Useful for obscure artists; results vary in quality so review before saving.
settings.web_search.title: Web Image Search
settings.webhooks.description: A webhook is an HTTP POST Stillwater sends to a URL you provide whenever a chosen event happens (a rule fixes a violation, an artist is added, and so on). Configure as many webhooks as you need to forward Stillwater events into Slack, Discord, your home automation, or any service that can accept JSON over HTTP.
settings.webhooks.help: Webhooks send HTTP POST requests to external services when events occur (scan complete, violations found, etc.). Generic webhooks send JSON payloads; Discord, Slack, and Gotify use service-specific formatting.
settings.webhooks.name.label: Webhook name
settings.webhooks.name.help: A label that identifies this webhook in the list. Only visible within Stillwater.
settings.webhooks.title: Webhooks
settings.webhooks.type.label: Webhook type
settings.webhooks.type.help: The payload format Stillwater uses when it sends the request. Generic sends a JSON object; Discord, Slack, and Gotify each use a format their service expects directly.
settings.webhooks.type_generic.descripti: Send a generic JSON payload describing the event. Use this for in-house tooling or services without a dedicated formatter.
settings.webhooks.url.label: Webhook URL
settings.webhooks.url.help: The HTTP or HTTPS endpoint that receives the POST request. For Discord, this is a Discord webhook URL; for Slack, an incoming webhook URL; for Gotify, the application push URL.
setup.choose_auth_method: Choose an authentication method for this Stillwater instance.
setup.how_to_sign_in: How do you want to sign in?
setup.restore_desc: Bring back a previous Stillwater installation from a passphrase-protected export file.
setup.restore_passphrase_hint: The passphrase you set when you exported the backup. Stillwater cannot recover it if you lose it.
setup.restore_subhead: Upload an export file and enter the passphrase to bring back your settings, libraries, providers, and local user accounts.
setup.start_fresh_desc: Create a new admin account and walk through the setup wizard from scratch.
tier_tooltip.free: No account or API key required
tier_tooltip.free_key: Free account required to obtain an API key
tier_tooltip.freemium: Free tier available with limits; paid tier unlocks more
prefs.drawer.title: Preferences
prefs.bg_opacity.flyout_desc: Flyout is hard-floored at 85% for readability. Full range available in stable Preferences.
prefs.bg_opacity.help: How opaque cards and the top bar are over the backdrop. The 85% floor keeps text AA-legible over the artwork; raise it toward 100% for fully solid surfaces. Lite mode forces opaque.
prefs.density.label: Layout Density
prefs.density.description: Controls vertical spacing in lists and grids. Compact fits more content; Spacious adds breathing room.
prefs.density.help: Vertical breathing room in lists and cards. Compact fits more on screen; Spacious adds padding.
prefs.mono_font.label: Monospace Font
prefs.mono_font.description: Used for keyboard badges, IDs, and timestamps.
prefs.mono_font.help: Used for IDs, timestamps, and code values, distinct from the body typeface. All options are system fonts or openly licensed (SIL OFL).
prefs.kbd_hints.label: Keyboard Hints
prefs.kbd_hints.description: Show or hide keyboard shortcut badges throughout the UI.
prefs.kbd_hints.help: The full ? shortcut cheat sheet stays available even when inline hints are hidden.
prefs.auto_fetch.label: Prefetch Images
prefs.auto_fetch.description: Automatically search providers for images when opening an artist's image page.
prefs.auto_fetch.help: Uses more bandwidth and storage up front in exchange for instant page loads.
prefs.notification_enabled.label: Notifications
prefs.notification_enabled.description: Show in-app toast notifications for rule violations.
prefs.notification_enabled.help: Affects in-app toasts only - not email or webhook notifications, which are configured in Settings.
prefs.show_platform_debug.label: Show Platform Debug Tab
prefs.show_platform_debug.description: Add a Debug tab to each artist page showing the raw API payload from connected platforms.
prefs.show_platform_debug.help: When on, a Debug tab appears on each artist's detail page with the raw API payload received from connected media servers (Emby, Jellyfin). Useful for diagnosing unexpected IDs, image URLs, or library paths.
prefs.sidebar_state.help: Full shows nav icons with labels. Icon-only reclaims horizontal space; Hidden removes the sidebar entirely.
prefs.content_width.help: Narrow caps line length for comfortable reading. Wide fills the screen to fit more per row.
prefs.thumbnail_size.help: Size of artist artwork in lists and the action queue. Shown here at the actual pixel sizes.
prefs.page_size.help: Rows per page on list screens (Artists, Reports). Server-side, so there is no live preview here; the value normalizes on blur.
prefs.font_family.help: Inter is the default. System uses your operating system's native font. Atkinson Hyperlegible boosts legibility for low-vision readers.
prefs.font_size.help: Scales text across the entire app.
prefs.letter_spacing.help: Tracks the whole UI. Extra Wide (0.05em) is a dyslexia-readability aid; Wide is 0.025em.
prefs.language.help: Sets the interface language. Biography language fallbacks are configured separately in Settings.
prefs.artist_layout.help: Drag the handle or use the arrows to reorder; the eye toggles a section's visibility. Hero and action bar are fixed page chrome and are not in the list. The chevron collapses or expands a section in place.
prefs.artist_layout.description: Drag to reorder sections. Toggle the eye to show or hide.
